macro_segments.title: Introduction to Ketamim (Stains) and the Debate Between Rabbi Meir and the Rabbis

micro_segments.title: Opening Statement: Ketamim as Rabbinic Concern and the Question of Attribution
Mishnah: Haro'ah Ketem - If a Woman Sees a Stain, She is Mekukeles and Must Be Concerned About Zov
Explanation of Mekukeles: How Stains Disrupt a Woman's Menstrual Cycle Counting
Rabbi Meir's Position: Concern About Three Days of Bleeding and Zavah Status
The Rabbis' Response: Rejecting the Concern for Zavah in Stains

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Chanina ben Antignos and the Conditions for Treating Stains as Zavah

micro_segments.title: Identification of the Rabbis: Rabbi Chanina ben Antignos's Position on Stains
Baraita: Three Pre-Checked Garments on Separate Days as Evidence of Zavah
Two Days of Direct Bleeding Plus a Third Day Stain: Overwhelming Evidence
Rava's Assessment: Rabbi Chanina ben Antignos Prevailed Over the Rabbis
Rabbi Chanina's Implicit Argument: Why Not Concern for Less Than Three Beans?

macro_segments.title: The Gris (Bean) Measurement and the Debate Over Stain Size Requirements

micro_segments.title: What is a Gris: A Measurement Used to Discount Smaller Stains Due to Lice
Three Gris as a Threshold: When Stains Must Be Treated as Multiple Days of Bleeding
Rabbi Chanina's Challenge: Can Three Gris Truly Indicate Three Separate Stains?
The Rabbis' Response: Dividing by Gris Size Per Day Justifies the Three-Day Assumption

macro_segments.title: Clarification of the Dispute: Location of Stains (Single Spot vs. Multiple Spots)

micro_segments.title: The Gemara's Reframing: Distinguishing Between Three Spots in One Garment vs. One Large Spot
Rabbi Chanina's Concession: Only Multiple Garments on Different Days Warrant Zavah Concern
The Final Settlement: Both Positions Acknowledge Different Thresholds of Evidence

macro_segments.title: Baraita on Stain Size: Three Gris and More Equals Zavah Concern

micro_segments.title: Tanu Rabbanan: Stain Divisible Into Three Gris Requires Zavah Concern (Majority View)
Rabbi Yehuda ben Agra in the Name of Rabbi Yosi: Even Two Gris Requires Concern
Rabbi Rebbi's Analysis: Stricter View Makes Sense Only Without Checking

macro_segments.title: The Bein Hashmashos Complication: Twilight and Double-Counting of Bleeding

micro_segments.title: The Possibility of Twilight Bleeding: Why Two Stains Could Count as Three Days
Rava Finds Yeshiva Students Discussing the Case: Checking Oneself During Twilight
Rabbi Yehuda vs. Rabbi Yosi's Twilight Periods: Nine Minutes vs. One Second
The Constructed Case: Checking During Rabbi Yehuda's Period But Not Rabbi Yosi's
How to Eliminate Bein Hashmashos: Continuous Self-Checking Throughout the Period

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Rebbi's Position on When Twilight Concern Applies

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Rebbi Concedes to Rabbi Yosi: Concern for Twilight When No Checking Was Done
Clarification: Rabbi Rebbi's Conditions for Accepting or Rejecting Twilight Concern
Implied Contradiction: Finding the Precise Case Both Positions Debate
Gemara Resolution: Both Sides Agree When No Checking Was Done at All

macro_segments.title: Reconciling Two Versions of Rabbi Yehuda ben Agra's Position

micro_segments.title: Baraita Contradiction: Large Stain vs. Smaller Stain and Zavah Concern
Rabbi Rebbi's Statement: Acknowledging Concern for Smaller Stains Under Certain Conditions
Gemara's Approach: Two Different Tannaim with Different Views on Twilight
Debate Over When Twilight Period Occurs: Whether It Is Continuous or Separate

macro_segments.title: Retroactive Assumption of Tumah: How Far Back Does a Stain Affect Previous Acts?

micro_segments.title: Rabbi's Position: Stains Render Woman and Sacrifices Impure Going Back to Last Washing
Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar: Stains Should Not Be Stricter Than Actual Seeing (24 Hours)
Gemara Challenge: Even Rabbi Shimon Admits Sacrifices Are Affected
Correction: Rabbi Shimon Extends Leniency to All Cases, Including Sacrifices

macro_segments.title: Connecting Stains to Subsequent Bleeding: The Twenty-Four Hour Rule

micro_segments.title: Baraita: If Stain Followed by Bleeding Within 24 Hours, Attribute Retroactively to Stain
Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar's Stricter Version: Bleeding Must Occur on Same Halachic Day
Rabbi Rebbi Acknowledges: Rabbi Shimon's View Is More Lenient and Helpful
Rav Nachman's Distinction: Both Views Have Different Consequences for Niddah vs. Zavah

macro_segments.title: Do Stains Require Hefsek Tahara (Checking Before Immersion)?

micro_segments.title: Rav Zeira's Question: Do Stains Require a Hefsek Tahara Before Mikvah?
Rav Assi's Silence: No Clear Answer Initially Provided
Later Realization: The Debate Between Reish Lakish and Rabbi Yochanan on Checking
Reish Lakish: Hefsek Tahara Required Only If Woman Actually Checked Monday Night
Rabbi Yochanan: Hefsek Tahara Required Regardless of Woman's Checking Action
Rav Assi's Recognition: Stains Do Indeed Require Hefsek Tahara, Surfacing in Learning Flow
